content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates specific, timely reads throughout. In turn 6, the coach names a precise linguistic read ('There's something in the way you said \"I thought maybe that was just maturity\" — like you're not quite convinced by your own explanation'), grounding the question in what was noticed rather than asking blind. In turn 8, the coach reads the pattern beneath the content (the defendedness/not-letting-anything-land). Most critically, in turn 10, the coach catches the pullback move ('even though you pulled it back') — naming the retraction itself as a read, not just the content of what was said. This is exactly the kind of distinguishing-the-said-from-the-shown read the rubric calls for, and it happens at the moment that changes the conversation. In turn 12, the coach makes a pattern-level read connecting the eight-months-ago 'graduation' to the disclosure now. Reads consistently precede questions.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a somatic question but without a named read yet."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Asks a topic question without naming what's being noticed."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Specific linguistic read: names the unconvinced tone in the client's own framing, then inquires. Read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern read of defendedness/not-letting-things-land, surfaced as a reframe."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the pullback ('even though you pulled it back') — reads the retraction itself, not just the content. Timely and specific."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern-level read linking the 'graduation' from therapy eight months ago to today's disclosure."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The rush signature wasn't strongly present in this client's pacing, so its absence isn't a real miss. The coach's strongest read — catching the pullback of the suicidal statement — is exactly the kind of distinguishing-said-from-shown read that defines a 3."
}
